briefdescription.para: This function is the handler which performs processing to handle data events from the device.

detaileddescription.para: It is called from an interrupt context. so the amount of processing should be minimal.
This handler provides an example of how to handle data for the device and is application specific.

parameterdescription.para: contains a callback reference from the driver, in this case it is the instance pointer for the XUartPs driver.
contains the specific kind of event that has occurred.
contains the number of bytes sent or received for sent and receive events.

briefdescription.para: This function handles the interrupt when data is in RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance

briefdescription.para: This function handles the interrupt when data has been sent, the transmit FIFO is empty (transmitter holding register).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance
is the register value for channel status register

briefdescription.para: This function handles the receive timeout interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: This interrupt occurs whenever a number of bytes have been present in the RX FIFO and the receive data line has been idle for at lease 4 or more character times, (the timeout is set using XUartPs_SetrecvTimeout() function).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance

detaileddescription.para: It does not do any processing except to call the application handler to indicate a modem event.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance

briefdescription.para: This function gets the interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

simplesect.para: The current interrupt mask. The mask indicates which interrupts are enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance
contains the interrupts to be enabled or disabled. A '1' enables an interrupt, and a '0' disables.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler that will be called when an event (interrupt) occurs that needs application's attention.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance
FuncPtr
is the pointer to the callback function.
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
None.
There is no assert on the CallBackRef since the driver doesn't know what it is (nor should it)

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the driver.

detaileddescription.para: It must be connected to an interrupt system by the application such that it can be called when an interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: contains a pointer to the driver instance
